text:
1. Instead of `❯ [tenex-edge] Incoming message mentioning this agent. Treat the following as input addressed to you in this session:

  Mention in testA from 09d48a1a at 2026-06-29 09:03 (just now)
  @developer hey there
  (message id: cd4db37e)` let's change it to `@developer hey there` [...] **① tmux + human + direct** — pasted as a real turn, minimal provenance, auto-reply:
```
<@pablo> @developer hey there
``` [...] If a) is not met we should show something like:

[tenex-edge mention] <@agent1> Hello @developer [...] **② tmux + agent + direct** — pasted as a real turn (agent mentions are *meant* to trigger execution):
```
[tenex-edge mention] <@agent1> Hello @developer
``` [...] Pasting an **agent's** mention into a tmux pane forces that agent to take a turn and auto-publish — two agents mentioning each other = an infinite ping-pong. Recommendation: **only whitelisted humans can drive an auto-turn via paste.** Agent→agent mentions surface as a framed `<tenex-edge>` block (like ambient, but flagged as a mention) that the agent *may* act on, rather than a forced turn. This keeps "humans are in charge" and kills the loop class for free. It also means case ② above becomes a framed block, not a pasted turn. [...] On the loop concern for ②: I'll respect that agent mentions trigger execution — no gating. Worth knowing the risk still exists (two agents mentioning each other = ping-pong), but the right fix if it ever bites is a soft consecutive-auto-turn depth limit, *not* suppressing delivery. I'll leave it out unless you want a safety valve. [...] One behavioral note to keep in mind on the agent-mention decision: form ② pastes as a turn and auto-publishes the reply, so two agents mentioning each other will ping-pong. As agreed, no gate — flag me if you later want a soft consecutive-auto-turn depth limit as a safety valve. [...] if b) is not met the hook-wrapped injection needs to show the message id since the agent needs it to reply:

<tenex-edge>
You were mentioned in #channel. Reply with `tenex-edge channel reply cd4db37e "message"`

<@pablo> @developer hey there!
</tenex-edge> [...] 
**③ hooks-only + direct** — framed, reply hint, no id:
```
<tenex-edge>
You were mentioned in #tenex-edge. Reply with: tenex-edge chat write "@pablo your reply"

<@pablo> @developer hey there!
</tenex-edge>
``` [...] 2. yes
3. yes, drop the message id
4. yes, I think the design should be, a message arrives to the inbox of a session, something drains it, either the tmux-wrapper that injects as a user message, or the hook -- if they are atomic they can't be reinjected twice with no explicit dedup gate [...] When a hooks-only turn has **both** a mention and background chatter, emit ③ then ④ as two blocks (mention is actionable, activity is FYI) rather than merging — keeps the "what must I act on" signal clean.
